---
title: "caladaptaer"
subtitle: "R access to Cal-Adapt climate projections"
---

The `caladaptaer` package provides native R access to Cal-Adapt climate data
from California's Fifth Climate Change Assessment. The data are downscaled
CMIP6 climate projections hosted on the
[Cal-Adapt Analytics Engine (CAE)](https://analytics.cal-adapt.org/). Given a
variable, model, scenario, and time window, `caladaptaer` reads the requested
subset and returns either a `stars` grid or a `data.frame`.

## What it does

- Discovers available models, scenarios, variables, and resolutions from the
  CAE catalog.
- Reads WRF and LOCA2 climate projections into R.
- Extracts time series for one or many locations.
- Builds CF-1.8 NetCDF meteorological driver files from hourly WRF data.

`caladaptaer` is a data access package. It does not model, bias-correct, or
analyze climate data. The objects it returns work directly with the standard R
geospatial and modeling stack.

## Available data

The package reads the two main downscaling products hosted by CAE. See
[Data Access](vignettes/data_access.qmd) for product-selection guidance, time
domains, and citations.

| Dataset | Resolution | Step | GCMs | Scenarios | Period |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| WRF CMIP6 (WUS-D3) | 45 / 9 / 3 km | Hourly | 8 | SSP3-7.0 (all); SSP2-4.5 and SSP5-8.5 (CESM2) | 1980--2100 |
| LOCA2-Hybrid | 3 km | Daily | 15 | SSP2-4.5, SSP3-7.0, SSP5-8.5 | 1950--2100 |

## Related Tools

| Tool | Use Case | How it differs from `caladaptaer` |
|---|---|---|
| [climakitae](https://climakitae.readthedocs.io/) | Python access and analysis workflows for CAE data | Official Python toolkit for the Fifth Assessment data; `caladaptaer` supports native R access. |
| [climakitaegui](https://climakitaegui.readthedocs.io/) | Interactive Python menus for retrieving CAE data | Adds a GUI-style layer on top of `climakitae`; `caladaptaer` supports scriptable R workflows. |
| [CAE intake catalog](https://analytics.cal-adapt.org/data/access/) | Direct Python/xarray/dask access to the CAE Zarr catalog | Lower-level Python access to the same cloud-hosted data; `caladaptaer` wraps discovery and fetch operations for R users. |
| [Cal-Adapt web tools](https://cal-adapt.org/) | Browser-based exploration, maps, charts, and downloads | Useful for visual exploration; `caladaptaer` is for reproducible R scripts and model workflows. |
| [caladaptR](https://ucanr-igis.github.io/caladaptr/) | R access to the legacy Cal-Adapt.org API | Targets the older Cal-Adapt API and Fourth Assessment-era datasets rather than the CAE cloud Zarr stores. |

## Guides and reference

| Page | What is on it |
|---|---|
| [Getting Started](vignettes/getting_started.qmd) | Minimal installation and point time-series workflow |
| [Data Access](vignettes/data_access.qmd) | Dataset background, product comparison, discovery, and fetch functions |
| [Met Drivers](vignettes/met_drivers.qmd) | WRF to CF NetCDF meteorological drivers |
| [Reference](reference/index.html) | Function documentation |

## Citing and license

`caladaptaer` is released under BSD-3-Clause. Please cite as

> B V, A., & LeBauer, D. (2026). caladaptaer: Access Cal-Adapt Climate Data from R (Version 0.2.0) [Computer software]. https://github.com/lebauerapproach/caladaptaer
